topStories.title: West Asia highlights: Trump says Hormuz to reopen Friday under U.S.-Iran deal
topStories.summary: Some of those details are still to be negotiated in talks over the next 60 days, though Trump said the memorandum of understanding would likely be released after it's signed on Friday

topStories.title: Investors fuel SpaceX boom as stock soars for second straight session
topStories.summary: SpaceX shares surged for a second day, adding to initial gains and pushing its market value over $2.3 trillion. This valuation places the company among the world's top six largest, nearing a challenge to the current leader. The company also exercised its over-allotment option, significantly increasing the total funds raised from its IPO.

topStories.title: Sebi plans buyback via SEs again, easier MF borrowing rules
topStories.summary: Sebi is set to overhaul buyback rules, potentially reintroducing stock exchange routes and easing merchant banker requirements. Mutual funds may also see relaxed intraday borrowing norms for broader cash management. These changes aim to simplify processes, reduce costs, and improve capital return for companies, while ensuring fairer shareholder participation and taxation.

topStories.title: Rupee also rises to 5-week high on peace deal hopes
topStories.summary: The Indian rupee reached a five-week high on Monday. Benchmark bond yields fell to their lowest since mid-April. This surge followed news of an initial peace deal in West Asia. Oil prices dropped significantly, easing pressure on India's financial assets. Traders are now optimistic about the rupee's future performance.

topStories.title: Sensex, Nifty rally 1% as US-Iran peace hopes spark risk-on sentiment
topStories.summary: Indian equities surged over 1% on Monday, with the Nifty 50 and Sensex climbing significantly. Hopes of a US-Iran peace deal and easing crude oil prices prompted traders to reduce bearish bets, lifting market sentiment. Realty, consumer durables, and auto stocks led the gains as investor anxiety eased.

topStories.title: Israeli Forces Won't Leave Lebanon, Syria, Gaza: Netanyahu After US-Iran Deal
topStories.summary: Netanyahu said that country's forces would remain in Lebanon, Gaza and Syria.

topStories.summary: There was no immediate information on whether anyone was hurt.

topStories.title: Heatwaves and ozone together increase India’s cardiac deaths: study
topStories.summary: A peer-reviewed study reports that surface ozone reaches 85-110 μg/m³ in northern India during heatwaves and exceeds the WHO guideline of 70 μg/m³ in every region of the country. In 2024, roughly 830 more deaths were reported during the heatwave than the preceding days.

topStories.title: Social media, video platforms surpass traditional outlets as leading news sources: report
topStories.summary: The report is based on an online survey of nearly 1 lakh people across 48 markets worldwide. The study states that consumption patterns have changed with audiences’ growing disengagement with news

topStories.title: US stocks: US market rallies, Dow ends with record on US-Iran deal, oil price slide
topStories.summary: Wall Street indexes surged Monday, with the Dow hitting a record high following a preliminary US-Iran agreement that eased inflation fears and sent oil prices plummeting. This relief rally boosted rate-sensitive tech stocks and energy-dependent sectors like airlines, while the Federal Reserve's upcoming policy update remains a key focus.

Authorities are focusing on onboarding more banks and simplifying procedures to enhance cross-border transaction efficiency.

business.title: Investors fuel SpaceX boom as stock soars for second straight session
business.summary: SpaceX shares surged for a second day, adding to initial gains and pushing its market value over $2.3 trillion. This valuation places the company among the world's top six largest, nearing a challenge to the current leader. The company also exercised its over-allotment option, significantly increasing the total funds raised from its IPO.

business.title: Sebi plans buyback via SEs again, easier MF borrowing rules
business.summary: Sebi is set to overhaul buyback rules, potentially reintroducing stock exchange routes and easing merchant banker requirements. Mutual funds may also see relaxed intraday borrowing norms for broader cash management. These changes aim to simplify processes, reduce costs, and improve capital return for companies, while ensuring fairer shareholder participation and taxation.

business.title: Rupee also rises to 5-week high on peace deal hopes
business.summary: The Indian rupee reached a five-week high on Monday. Benchmark bond yields fell to their lowest since mid-April. This surge followed news of an initial peace deal in West Asia. Oil prices dropped significantly, easing pressure on India's financial assets. Traders are now optimistic about the rupee's future performance.

business.title: Sensex, Nifty rally 1% as US-Iran peace hopes spark risk-on sentiment
business.summary: Indian equities surged over 1% on Monday, with the Nifty 50 and Sensex climbing significantly. Hopes of a US-Iran peace deal and easing crude oil prices prompted traders to reduce bearish bets, lifting market sentiment. Realty, consumer durables, and auto stocks led the gains as investor anxiety eased.

business.title: US stocks: US market rallies, Dow ends with record on US-Iran deal, oil price slide
business.summary: Wall Street indexes surged Monday, with the Dow hitting a record high following a preliminary US-Iran agreement that eased inflation fears and sent oil prices plummeting. This relief rally boosted rate-sensitive tech stocks and energy-dependent sectors like airlines, while the Federal Reserve's upcoming policy update remains a key focus.

business.title: India, UAE step up efforts to expand re-dirham trade
business.summary: Over 15% of India-UAE trade has been invoiced in local currencies since the rupee-dirham mechanism launched nearly three years ago. Both nations are now intensifying efforts to boost adoption, aiming for faster trade and cost reduction, despite industry-cited structural bottlenecks. Authorities are focusing on onboarding more banks and simplifying procedures to enhance cross-border transaction efficiency.

business.title: Govt to sell up to 5% stake in insurer GIC via OFS
business.summary: The Indian government is set to divest up to a 5% stake in General Insurance Corporation. This offer for sale begins for non-retail investors on June 16 and for retail investors on June 17. The floor price is fixed at 352 Indian rupees per share. This move is part of a larger plan to meet public shareholding norms.

business.title: Angel One settles Sebi proceedings over lapses in monitoring authorised persons, pays Rs 4.28 crore
business.summary: Angel One has settled Sebi's adjudication and enquiry proceedings by paying Rs 4.28 crore. The regulator had alleged supervisory and monitoring lapses involving two authorised persons, including failures in due diligence, inspection processes, fund collection oversight and scrutiny of unauthorised activities.

business.title: Remittances, services surplus lift India to $4.7 billion current account surplus in April
business.summary: India achieved a significant current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April. This positive shift was driven by robust inward remittance flows. The services sector also contributed to the surplus. However, higher imports, particularly crude oil, led to a merchandise deficit. Foreign portfolio investors withdrew funds amid global conflicts, impacting the capital account.
